new_classes.definition_en: Residual category of spirituous beverages of heading 2208 not classified elsewhere under subheading 2208 90, including: (1) spirituous beverages with added aerated water (e.g. whisky-soda); (2) tea with alcohol; (3) spirituous beverages mixed with each other or with fruit or vegetable juices; and the types listed in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2208, third paragraph, items (14) to (18). Presented in containers of any size not covered by more specific subheadings.
A beverage consisting of tea to which alcohol (ethyl alcohol or a spirituous beverage) has been added, classifiable under CN 2208 90 69.

new_classes.label_en: spirituous beverage with added aerated water
new_classes.definition_en: A mixture of a spirituous beverage and aerated water (e.g. whisky-soda), where the aerated water is added to the spirit, resulting in a mixed spirituous drink classifiable under CN 2208 90 69.

new_classes.label_en: mixture of spirituous beverages or spirituous beverages mixed with fruit or vegetable juice
new_classes.label_de: Gemisch aus alkoholhaltigen Getränken oder Gemisch aus alkoholhaltigen Getränken mit Frucht- oder Gemüsesäften
new_classes.definition_en: A beverage produced by mixing two or more spirituous beverages together, or by mixing a spirituous beverage with fruit or vegetable juice of heading 2009, classifiable under CN 2208 90 69.

coverage_explanation: The legal text identifies several specific product types under CN 2208 90 69: (1) spirituous beverages with added aerated water (e.g. whisky-soda) — covered via SpiritousBeverageWithAeratedWater with a BFO part restriction on AeratedWater; (2) tea with alcohol — covered via AlcoholicTeaBeverage; (3) spirituous beverages mixed together or with fruit/vegetable juices — covered via SpiritousBeverageMixture with hasIngredient restriction on FruitOrVegetableJuice2009. The legal text also references HS Explanatory Note paragraph 3 items (14)–(18), which enumerate further specific spirit types (e.g. Okole­hao, Calvados substitutes, Shochu, Soju, etc.); these cannot be individually captured without the full HS note text and are not covered here, reducing coverage. The overarching OtherSpiritousBeverage22089069 class captures the residual 'other' nature of the code. The complement restriction pattern used to distinguish from 22089056 is structurally weak as a discrimination tool (it references a property rather than a sibling class disjointness), reducing the score slightly. Tea ingredient modelling via hasBaseIngredient self-reference is a placeholder; a proper TeaIngredient class would improve coverage but would require additional TBox terms.
